texto: You have to believe in yourself and only trust your own vision and instincts. If I'd listened to what other people thought about my work in the first 10 years that I was a writer, I never would have made it to begin with.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Philipp Meyer sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “You have to believe in yourself and only trust your own vision and instincts. If I'd listened to …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a trabalho, work, vision, útil em momentos de trabalho e vision.

frase_original: You have to believe in yourself and only trust your own vision and instincts. If I'd listened to what other people thought about my work in the first 10 years that I was a writer, I never would have made it to begin with.

texto: The white people are crazy. They all want to be rich, same as we do, but they do not admit to themselves that you only get rich by taking things from other people. They think that if you do not see the people you are stealing from, or if you do not know them, or if they do not look like you, it is not really stealing.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Philipp Meyer: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “The white people are crazy. They all want to be rich, same as we do, but they do not admit to the…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, race, people pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: The white people are crazy. They all want to be rich, same as we do, but they do not admit to themselves that you only get rich by taking things from other people. They think that if you do not see the people you are stealing from, or if you do not know them, or if they do not look like you, it is not really stealing.
